name: Messiah (Mashiach)
shortDescription: In Jewish eschatology, the Messiah is a future Jewish king from the Davidic line, who is expected to save the Jewish nation and inaugurate a Messianic Age of peace and prosperity for all humankind.
description: The concept of the Messiah (Mashiach in Hebrew, meaning 'anointed one') is a central tenet of Jewish eschatology. The Messiah is envisioned as a future Jewish leader, a king descended from the lineage of King David, who will redeem the Jewish people and usher in an era of universal peace, justice, and understanding. The Messianic Age is characterized by the rebuilding of the Temple in Jerusalem, the ingathering of the Jewish exiles to the Land of Israel, and a heightened awareness of God's presence in the world. While different interpretations exist within Judaism regarding the Messiah's specific role and the precise nature of the Messianic Age, the core belief remains a powerful source of hope and inspiration. Some emphasize the Messiah as a political and military leader who will restore Jewish sovereignty, while others focus on the Messiah as a spiritual guide who will bring about moral and ethical transformation. The coming of the Messiah is often associated with miraculous events and profound changes in the natural world. The belief in the Messiah is not merely a passive expectation but also an active call to action, urging Jews to live righteous lives and work towards the betterment of society, thereby hastening the arrival of the Messianic Age. The concept has significantly influenced other religions, particularly Christianity, which identifies Jesus as the Messiah, and Islam, which includes Isa (Jesus) as a prophet who will return before the Day of Judgment.

sources.title: Mishneh Torah, Kings and Their Wars, Chapter 11
sources.author: Maimonides
